extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won his fourth consecutive sprint stage at the Tour with VAN DER POEL's extended leadout while CAVENDISH showed revival speed but insufficient positioning.
tactical_patterns: PHILIPSEN's Alpecin extends leadout beyond 1km using VAN DER POEL, forcing rivals to burn matches early chasing position.
Early breakaway attempts self-collapsed when team cars recalled riders, suggesting directors pre-decided sprint finish was inevitable.
CAVENDISH positioned mid-pack over 1km out requires luck versus positioning, indicating Astana leadout capacity deficit against Alpecin numbers.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Four consecutive Tour sprint wins including late-2022 carry-over reveals unprecedented consistency window for sprint dominance
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: Extended final kilometer leadout role demonstrates willingness to sacrifice GC positioning for sprint train superiority
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: neutral: Raw sprint speed competitive for stage win but positioned too far back at 1km, suggesting team infrastructure gap
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: neutral: Fought onto PHILIPSEN's wheel through aggressive positioning but lacked final acceleration to contest
rider_signals.GUGLIELMI Simon: neutral: Archaea allowed solo continuation after multi-rider break collapsed, revealing team willingness to burn domestique for publicity
